score_note: Defines dominance, cells, rooms, and doors accurately, but does not connect the refinements to the room-door parity setup.
Identifies the minimum-image representation, but does not explicitly derive sigma.card <= C.card or its later cardinality role.
Distinguishes the point-insertion and color-insertion constructors, but omits their door-room incidence-counting role.
Explains colorful, nearly colorful, and typed missing color, but misses the fixed-type parity-counting purpose.
Mentions colorful implies room and point extraction, but not the nonempty/cardinality sandwich needed for pick_colorful_point.
Identifies typed door-room incidences and outside/internal parity, but misses the room-side split into colorful versus non-colorful rooms.
Correctly explains that the outside-door filter is a singleton and hence has odd cardinality.
States that parity gives oddness of the colorful-room filter, but does not explain the odd-plus-even count equation or nonemptiness extraction.
Misstates the theorem as a tautological existence claim and gives an incorrect reason for Inhabited I/default.
Connects Scarf to grid-level colorful rooms and room_point_seq, but does not explain the Fcolor coordinate choice.
Mentions a finite-image constant subsequence, but confuses colors with the room index set and misses why fixed C is needed for estimates.
Correctly says outside-C coordinates tend to zero, but does not connect this to inequalities on C and the final simplex equality.
Mentions compactness and vanishing diameter, but only vaguely explains how diameter transfers room-level inequalities to the limit.
Mentions coordinate inequalities and limiting behavior, but misses the colored witnesses and continuity/diameter transfer mechanism.
Uses some formal names and convergence language, but omits the discretization, Scarf colorful rooms, and shrinking-diameter bridge.
Mentions pushing toward the uniform point, but misses block deficits, positive block sums, and projection normalization.
Correctly identifies projection after embedding as the left inverse/retraction needed to convert the lifted fixed point into a product fixed point.
Describes the lifted map and continuity, but omits the projection-back and project_embed_id retraction step.
Only describes finite players and pure strategy types; it misses mixed profiles as products of simplices, expected payoff, and no-deviation.
Explains normalization and continuity for Brouwer, but does not clearly identify the payoff-improvement construction.
Mentions a fixed point of nash_map leading to mixedNashEquilibrium, but misses pure-deviation contradiction and mixed_g_linear.
Correctly connects internal_door_two_rooms to size-two fibers and even internal-door contribution.
Recognizes the nearly-colorful/colorful dichotomy, but does not preserve the same typed missing color through the incidence.
Mentions two doors/fibers for non-colorful nearly colorful rooms, but incorrectly suggests the whole dbcountingset has cardinality 2.
Correctly explains TT as the finite integer grid, TTtostdSimplex division by l, and indexed orders for Scarf.
Mentions coordinate bounds and the two named limit lemmas, but does not clearly explain rescaling to diameter shrinkage and outside-C vanishing.
Explains extracting a subsequence with constant C, but not how fixed C supports outside-C and inside-C estimates.
Mentions coordinate inequalities/equality, but bypasses the simplex sum-one argument that forces equality.
Explains flat-to-block indexing, but omits inverse lemmas and their role in block sums and retraction proofs.
Identifies blockWeight scaling, but does not explain whole-simplex sum one and projection recovery.
Correctly explains that projection divides by blockSum and needs a positive nonzero denominator for continuity.
Sets up the tPush case split, but misses the zero-deficit argument in the tPush = 0 branch.
States the linear decomposition of mixed deviations, but does not explain the convex-average bound from pure deviations.
Explains that reindexing and simplex equivalences transport the finite game space to the Brouwer-compatible representation and back.
Incorrectly says simplex validity is not explicitly proved and relies on continuity, missing nonnegativity, denominator positivity, and sum-one normalization.
Mentions fixedness and inequalities, but not the key contradiction between sum_g > 1 and sum_g = 1.
Correctly explains outside doors as empty tau with singleton color and the singleton-counting consequence.
Correctly says the two-room fact is packaged as a size-two fiber, giving even internal-door count.
Correctly combines outside-door-to-typed-nearly-colorful with classification of adjacent rooms as typed nearly colorful or colorful.
Does not explain odd cardinality, positive cardinality/nonempty filter, or default-based extraction of an element.
Mentions stdSimplex.pick and the coordinate inequality, but gives the wrong reason for existence and adds an irrelevant distinctness claim.
Identifies fixed C and subsequence g1, but omits their later inside-C/outside-C proof roles.
Identifies y_seq as a colored witness idea, but does not clearly explain the y_i <= f(y)_i inequality and transfer to z by diameter plus continuity.
Mentions outside-C zero and nonnegativity, but does not reconstruct the sum-one mass argument that forces coordinate equality.
Names the two endpoint roles of C, but does not explain how the inside/outside split yields extensional equality f z = z.
Mentions positivity and blockWeight, but misses why zero deficit is needed when the positive push term vanishes.
Correctly identifies division by blockSum and the need for positivity/nonzero denominator in project_continuous.
Correctly connects embedded block sums to deficit zero, push identity, and recovery of y by projection.
Correctly explains applying single-simplex Brouwer to f_lifted on BigSimplex, projecting back, and using project_embed_id.
Correctly identifies max 0 as the positive payoff-improvement term from pure deviations, giving nonnegative weights for normalization.
Explains denominator positivity from one_le_sum_g, but misses nonnegativity and the sum-one/nash_map_cert packaging.
Mentions linearity and pure strategies, but not the weighted-sum bound showing pure-deviation bounds imply mixed-deviation bounds.
Correctly states the contradiction between H1's sum_g > 1 and fixedness/self_div_lemma forcing sum_g = 1.
Recognizes reindexing via phi/phi_inv for fixed-point transport, but misstates the issue as infinite-dimensional and misses ProductSimplices.
